headline: Chief Operating Officer - DOOH.com
summary:
I am a natural problem solver who has worked across multiple industries from digital advertising/production to criminal investigation. With over 5 years of senior management experience I have helped to shape a leading production agency in digital out of home.

I have worked on hundreds of campaigns (some multi awards winners). I’m a natural leader and approach work with an open mind. I am a diversity advocate and was Creative Director on the multi award winning Pride in London #FreedomTo campaign.

In January 2020 I was promoted to Chief Operating Officer at DOOH.com after working with the company from 2014 when it was founded. IAs part of this role I lead the day to day business of DOOH.com. This includes managing our operations and business development in the UK, Ireland and around the world after helping to successfully establish DOOH.com as an industry leader in the UK.

experiences.title: Chief Operating Officer
experiences.description:
As COO I run the day to day elements of the business, strategy and management reporting directly to the CEO. I oversee the operations, production and commercial sides of the business. 

Three department heads report into me and I lead our key relationships with partners and clients. In the last six months I have implemented significant changes to the business to allow growth. I have changed how we work with our key clients, recruiting a new commercial lead and building new relationships with clients' senior management team to allow us to secure future work and create new revenue stream through products we develop. This has all allowed us to exceed targets and improve our overall working relationships.

I work on new business and in 2019 Q4 I worked to secure a new global client for the management of content across 11 cities (10 countries) for one of the largest events in the world.

experiences.title: Managing Partner
experiences.description:
As managing partner I handle the day to day when it comes the operations and logistics. I manage the two production team heads and oversee production in general. In the last year I have set up our office in Ireland so we could carry out dynamic digital out of home production in the growing OOH market in Ireland. 

In early 2019 I moved DOOH.com to our brand new larger offices in Fitzrovia. This has allowed us to expand out growing team and give staff and the company a more comfortable and fitting environment. As we have expanded over the years I have helped to change our organisational design and implementing a new structure.

experiences.title: Head of Operations & Production
experiences.description:
As Head of Operations & Production at DOOH.com I oversaw the day to day operations and lead production. 

I oversaw all projects that the teams worked on including work allocation. I also lead on some of the larger projects and worked directly with our clients. 

During my time in this role I grew the team to employing over 5 extra people. I also implemented testing in house of all of our projects and work and built relationships with all our partners (media owners etc).

experiences.title: Senior Producer
experiences.description:
As senior producer for DOOH.com I lead projects for one of the UK's fastest growing, out of home production companies. We drive innovation through everything we do, on screen, through delivery which is both efficient and cost effective so our clients budgets are released to achieve more reach. 

I was Senior Producer and Account Manager for DOOH.com for two years. I worked on multiple large scale projects including Yahoo, Oral B, Elle, Coca Cola, Twitter (to name a few) across Digital Out of Home throughout the country and abroad.

In 2016 our campaign for CALM with Kinetic won over 40 awards including a Cannes Lion Silver. This campaign awards wins were a proud moment for us all as the campaigned for better understanding and prevention of male suicide in the UK.

experiences.title: Creative Director - FreedomTo Advertising Campaign
experiences.description:
Voluntary Position
Creative Director for the Pride in London advertising campaign that was shown on London buses and the underground in the two weeks prior to Pride. 
This role involved:
- Working with Exterion on ad specifications, guidelines and submission.
- Liaising regularly with Barclays Bank who are the main corporate sponsor of Pride in London
- Designing/Creating mock ups and pitching to Barclays Bank, Exterion, The Board of Pride of London and the Citizen Advisory Board
- Liaising with celebrity agents and participants of the campaign.
- Directing photo/video for the content of the campaign.
- Signing off all final art work.
The campaign ran on 400 London Buses, all London Underground Digital Escalator Panels and all London Underground Cross Track Projectors in the two weeks prior to Pride in London 2014. This had a potential view of around 10 million people.
The campaign also ran on the Trafalgar Square screens next to the main stage throughout Pride and on the FreedomTo bus (double decker bus with a screen on the entire side of the bus) which went in the parade. This had a potential view of over 750,000 people. 
The creative concept of the campaign was designed by Myself and Matt McSherry in partnership with Enigma (now DOOH.com). The campaign went live on the 16th June 2014.

The campaign won several awards including Stonewall's Advert of the Year.

experiences.title: Constable
experiences.description:
Child Protection Investigator (2013 - 2014) 

Previous experience:
Investigator (2012 - 2013)
- Crime scene management for serious crimes
- Carrying out primary and secondary investigations of complex crimes.
Achievements:
- Investigated serious assaults and secured charges and convictions.

Proactive Crime Squad - London Riots (2011 - 2012)
- Carry out reviews of all CCTV viewed.
- Create and manage identification database.
- Distribute Images of possible suspects internally and externally.
- Manage staff members working on the CCTV review team
- Attend court daily during trials as a liaison between the Police the CPS/Defence
- Manage, create and distribute evidential packs to the court. 
- Create compilations of all evidential footage for use in Crown Court.
Achievements:
- Worked on the London Riots Investigation which secured over 25 convictions for violent disorder.
- Project managed the CCTV identification. This involved identifying over 150 unknown suspects on over 600 hours of CCTV footage.
- Acted as the Officer in the Case for two Crown Court trials
- Project managed the presentation of evidence for two Crown Court trials.

Sector Intelligence Officer (2010 - 2012)
- Carry out daily crime analysis
- Devise and manage multiple complex covert/over operations
- Manage other sector teams and carry out appraisals
- Attend operation review meetings with senior managers 
- Write crime analysis and operation reports and present them to senior management
Achievements:
- Devised and managed over 30 complex intelligence operations/strategies to reduce crime generators (covert and overt).
- Worked closely with Multi Agency Partners to achieve crime reduction (including housing, RSPCA, YOT, IOM, BPI) strengthening partnership working on the borough and improved information sharing.
- Carried out training of staff members on handling restricted information and intelligence submission.

Uniformed Constable (2008-2010)

experiences.title: Creative Director - Barclays PingIt Campaign
experiences.description:
Freelance
Creative Director for the Barclays PingIt campaign that was shown on the main stage screens in Trafalgar Square on the 28th June 2014 during Pride in London.
This role involved:
- Working with Barclays on ad specifications, guidelines and submission.
- Liaising regularly with Barclays Bank who are the main corporate sponsor of Pride in London
- Creating mock ups and pitching to Barclays Bank.
- Signing off all final art work.

experiences.title: Crime Prevention/ASB Reduction Officer
experiences.description:
Primary responsibility: 
Working on crime reduction projects. Working closely with Transport Crime and ASB Prevention Managers to assist in the programming and delivery of crime prevention and ASB reduction initiatives
Regular duties:
- Carrying out designing out security surveys on London Underground Station.
- Carrying out assessments on crime reduction initiatives across the transport network.
Achievements:
- Carried out crime surveys and created a database to analyse current security of all London Underground Stations.

experiences.title: Lead Group Youth Worker
experiences.description: Managing a weekly LGBT youth group

experiences.company: Western Dorset Crime & Disorder Reduction Partnership
experiences.title: ASB Caseworker
experiences.description:
Primary responsibility: 
Carry out investigations into all reports of anti social behaviour (ASB) across West Dorset and to manage community projects to reduce ASB and crime. 
Regular duties:
- Investigate cases of ASB/low level crime.
- Manage case conferences with multi agency partners across West Dorset.
- Devise and manage large scale projects to reduce ASB/low level crime.
- Manage project budgets.
- Write reports and carry out analysis on ASB and crime trends to present to senior managers.
- Supervision and management of other staff members and multi agency partners.
Achievements:
- Investigated over 100 cases of ASB/crime 
- Implemented 15 Acceptable Behaviour Contracts, 4 Civil Anti Social Behaviour Orders, 4 Dispersal Orders.
- Devised and managed several community projects including; Graffiti Removal Project working in partnership with the local authority, Dorset Police and Probation. 
- Chaired several large community meetings to give advise on ASB And low level crime reduction.

experiences.title: Accounts Administrator
experiences.description:
Primary responsibility: 
Managing the accounts of the local branch on a daily basis.
Regular Duties:
- Handling large amounts of cash on a daily basis.
- Improving the accounts database
- Ensuring the accounts of the local branch were in order
Achievements:
- Improved and streamlined the accounts database to enable easier data inputting and analysis

experiences.title: Product Purchasing Controller
experiences.description:
Primary responsibility: 
Create and manage a purchasing department at the growing branch. Carry out all product purchasing.
Regular Duties:
- Create a purchasing database.
- Devise and  implement new company policy for purchasing products.
- Reduce waste and returns.
- Carry out regular stock audits.
- Manage warehouse staff.
- Negotiate pricing with suppliers.
Achievements:
- Created a new purchasing department.
- Created a database for all stock purchasing and returns. The database allowed easy inputting and automatically created detailed reports. The entire database was created from scratch.
- Wrote the new purchasing policies.
- Wrote a streamlined audit procedure that allowed quicker auditing and rectification.

accomplishment_publications.name: CampaignLive
accomplishment_publications.description: Pride in London 2014 launches its first ever advertising campaign.

accomplishment_honors_awards.title: Cannes Lion 2016 - Silver
accomplishment_honors_awards.description: The #BiggerIssues campaign for CALM and Lynx we worked on with TMW, Kinetic, Mindshare and W Communications won a Silver at Cannes Lions. The #BiggerIssues campaign was launched to highlight male suicide. Every 2 hours a man ends their life. The campaign highlighted this on DOOH by updating headlines every two hours to reflect what was trending on social media and the fact that more people were talking about that rather than male suicide. The campaign went on to be talked about in Parliament and even got the attention of Prince William recently.

accomplishment_honors_awards.title: COOH - Digital Advert / Advertising Award

accomplishment_honors_awards.description: For DOOH.com's Heathrow Express - Comparison Generator campaign

accomplishment_honors_awards.title: Digital Out of Home Planning Award

volunteer_work.title: Senior Manager - Communications (Recruitment & Retention)
volunteer_work.description: Voluntary Position - Lead for volunteer communications and engagement online. - Primary liaison for volunteers on Facebook and twitter. - Editor of Volunteer Newsletters, updates to over 2000 subscribers via email. - Implementation of any online content required in relation to recruiting/retaining volunteers. At the beginning of this role we were asked to start a new way to recruit volunteers who would work for Pride on the main pride day. We started with a database of around 1200 people and increased it to over 2600 people. I implemented using a active Facebook group that we used to liaise with our volunteers on a daily basis. This went from no members to over 550 in three months. We increased the volunteers who worked at Pride in London from 400 to 600 and had a satisfaction rate of over 90%.

volunteer_work.title: Communications Manager
volunteer_work.description: Voluntary Elected Position Responsible for IT & Communications and Social Media for London Raiders Softball Club. I'm currently in my second year in this position. In our second year we have already designed a brand new website and relaunched their communications.

volunteer_work.title: Special Constable
